title: Leslie And Ben

jokes.text: Let's get married. Tonight.
jokes.setup: Romantic moment at a gala with standard engagement energy
jokes.punchline: Sudden escalation to immediate marriage plans
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie impulsiveness - character-driven comedy that feels authentic while being absurd

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Ben, I love you so much. I want to spend the rest of my life with you.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: I love you too. Leslie, will you marry me?

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Yes! Oh my God, yes!

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Great. I'm thinking next Saturday.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Next Saturday? Ben, that's six days away.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: I know. Is that too soon?

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: No, it's perfect. I'll call the venue tomorrow.

jokes.text: And your parents aren't here. - Good. Yeah, that makes it better.
jokes.setup: Listing obstacles to getting married tonight
jokes.punchline: Ben's relief that his parents won't be there
jokes.explanation: Good character moment revealing Ben's family dynamics with efficient delivery

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: We don't have a venue, we don't have a cake, we don't have rings, we don't have flowers, we don't have a dress, we don't have officiants—

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: My parents aren't coming.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: I'm just saying, there's a silver lining to all this.

jokes.text: And we just ordered 200 white-chocolate top hats. - We did? - You did, actually.
jokes.setup: Leslie listing wedding preparations already made
jokes.punchline: Absurd specificity of white-chocolate top hats and Ben's obliviousness
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie over-the-top planning with perfect absurd detail that reveals character

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: We've already ordered the white-chocolate top hats for the groomsmen, hired a string quartet that only plays songs in B-flat, and booked a calligrapher to hand-write all 847 place cards.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Wait, white-chocolate top hats? Those are going to melt.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: That's the point, Ben. They're *supposed* to melt. It's a metaphor for the fleeting nature of our wedding day.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Or we could just... not have hats made of food?

jokes.text: or July 57th
jokes.setup: Ben listing possible wedding dates
jokes.punchline: Impossible date of July 57th delivered casually
jokes.explanation: Perfect throwaway absurdist moment with maximum economy - gets laugh without drawing attention

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: We could do July 57th.

jokes.text: You're either in or your out, buddy. It was my idea, I'm totally in.
jokes.setup: Leslie's intense commitment demand
jokes.punchline: Ben pointing out it was his idea while affirming commitment
jokes.explanation: Solid character beat showing Leslie's intensity and Ben's patient response

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: I need to know you're 100% committed to this. No backing out, no matter what.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Leslie, I was the one who suggested it in the first place.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Of course I'm committed.

jokes.text: We can't look at each other! Well, you are aware that we've seen each other several times today.
jokes.setup: Leslie suddenly worried about wedding superstitions
jokes.punchline: Ben's logical observation about already seeing each other
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Leslie's superstitious panic and Ben's rationality

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Wait, we can't see each other before the wedding! That's bad luck!

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Leslie, we live together.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: I know, but it's bad luck!

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: You literally just saw me getting out of the shower.

jokes.text: I really want you to take my last name... that Leslie Knope disappears and becomes Leslie Wyatt. Or Councilwoman Mrs. Ben Wyatt.
jokes.setup: Ben bringing up name change topic seriously
jokes.punchline: Absurd suggestion that Leslie give up her identity/career
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection that builds perfectly to absurd conclusion of erasing Leslie's identity

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Leslie, I've been thinking... maybe you should change your last name to Wyatt.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: What? Ben, no. I'm Leslie Knope. I've built my entire career as Leslie Knope.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: I know, I know. But think about it — you could be Leslie Wyatt. You could give up your job, your accomplishments, your identity as we know it, and just become Mrs. Ben Wyatt.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: That's insane.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Is it though?

jokes.text: Wait, are you messing with me? - Sorry, I had to give it a shot.
jokes.setup: Ben's serious delivery of the name change demand
jokes.punchline: Reveal that he was joking and testing her
jokes.explanation: Solid payoff to the misdirection with good character moment

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Leslie, I need you to change your last name to Wyatt. I'm serious.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: What? Ben, no. I'm not changing my name.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: I'm just kidding. I was testing you.

jokes.text: Wait. Where do I go? Run!
jokes.setup: Leslie frantically sending Ben away
jokes.punchline: Ben's confusion about where to run followed by Leslie just shouting 'Run!'
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy moment with good character dynamics

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Go! Run! Get out of here!

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Where am I running to?

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: RUN!

jokes.text: The falcon is entering! The turtledove acknowledges!
jokes.setup: Secret operation planning for the wedding
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic spy code names for wedding planning
jokes.explanation: Pure Leslie behavior - treating wedding planning like a military operation

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Okay, from this moment forward, we go dark. We are now Operation Wedding Bells.

dialogue.speaker: Ann
dialogue.line: Leslie, it's just wedding planning.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Negative. You are now Agent Bridesmaid. I am Agent Groom Protector. We communicate only in code.

dialogue.speaker: Ann
dialogue.line: This is ridiculous.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: The flowers have arrived. I repeat, the flowers have arrived. Initiating Phase Two.

jokes.text: the sensuality of Eleanor Roosevelt combined with the animal magnetism of Lesley Stahl
jokes.setup: Leslie describing her desired wedding look
jokes.punchline: Bizarre combination of political figures as beauty inspiration
jokes.explanation: Perfectly specific Leslie absurdity - only she would want to look like political journalists

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: I want to look like a cross between Margaret Thatcher, Ronald Reagan, and Golda Meir. Like, Thatcher's hair, Reagan's tan, and Meir's pantsuit.

jokes.text: I feel like every crazy thing Leslie's ever had me do has been, like a drill, and today is the real thing. I'm ready, because I had the greatest teacher in the world. Leslie. Who is crazy.
jokes.setup: Ann describing how Leslie prepared her for this moment
jokes.punchline: Sweet sentiment undercut by calling Leslie crazy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ann moment - supportive but realistic about Leslie's intensity

dialogue.speaker: Ann
dialogue.line: Leslie prepared me for this moment. She said, 'Ann, you're going to be amazing. You're smart, you're beautiful, you're going to change the world.' It was so sweet.

dialogue.speaker: Ann
dialogue.line: Then she grabbed my shoulders and said, 'But if you mess this up, I will actually lose my mind. Like, certifiably insane. They will have to medicate me.'

jokes.text: That guy's my wife. - Um, you also work there.
jokes.setup: Andy saying he has a guy at City Hall
jokes.punchline: April pointing out Andy also works there
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy logic failure with April's deadpan correction

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I have a guy at City Hall.

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: You work at City Hall.

jokes.text: It would be an honor, and the first time I won't regret walking down the aisle.
jokes.setup: Leslie asking Ron to walk her down the aisle
jokes.punchline: Ron's dig at his own failed marriages
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron humor - sweet moment undercut by self-deprecating marriage joke

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: I would be honored. I've walked three women down the aisle before. They all left me, but I have no doubt that Ben will stay with you.

jokes.text: Do you want more like a Steve Harvey vibe or straight-up Seacrest? Never mind. I'll combine 'em-- Steve-crest.
jokes.setup: Tom planning his wedding officiant style
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous portmanteau of Steve Harvey and Ryan Seacrest
jokes.explanation: Very Tom behavior - pop culture mashup delivered with confidence

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: I'm gonna be like Steve Harvey and Ryan Seacrest combined. I'll call myself Stevacrest.

jokes.text: There is some beautiful jewelry in here. But be careful. There's also nails.
jokes.setup: Directing them to jewelry section
jokes.punchline: Casual warning about actual nails mixed with jewelry
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist moment with perfect deadpan delivery of weird situation

dialogue.speaker: Store clerk
dialogue.line: The jewelry section is over there. Fair warning though - we just got a shipment of actual nails mixed in with the costume jewelry, so watch out for those.

jokes.text: And they were nipple rings, at one point. How do you know that? They were his.
jokes.setup: Suggesting rings that look nice
jokes.punchline: Revealing they're Andy's former nipple rings
jokes.explanation: Great escalating cringe moment that's perfectly in character for Andy

dialogue.speaker: Store clerk
dialogue.line: These rings look really nice.

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: Those are Andy's former nipple rings.

jokes.text: I guess I better commit as many crimes as possible tonight. Get it out of my system.
jokes.setup: Andy mentioning he's becoming a cop tomorrow
jokes.punchline: Backwards logic about committing crimes before becoming police
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy logic - completely backwards reasoning delivered with confidence

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I'm becoming a cop tomorrow, so tonight I'm gonna commit as many crimes as possible.

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: That's not how that works.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Once I'm a cop, I can't break the law anymore. So I gotta get it all out of my system tonight.

jokes.text: I don't want anyone else in this town to get married, because it makes it less special for us.
jokes.setup: April taking all the marriage licenses
jokes.punchline: Selfishly wanting to prevent other weddings
jokes.explanation: Very April - selfish and dark but delivered matter-of-factly

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: I'm going to take all the marriage licenses.

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: I'm not being selfish. I just want to prevent other people from getting married.

jokes.text: If you're not gonna finish something three months early, then don't accept the job.
jokes.setup: Dress isn't finished yet
jokes.punchline: Leslie's unreasonable timeline expectations
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie perfectionism taken to absurd extreme

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: We need this dress finished by tomorrow morning.

dialogue.speaker: Seamstress
dialogue.line: Tomorrow morning? That's impossible. I need at least a week.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: A week? I need it by 6 AM. I have a breakfast meeting at 6:15.

jokes.text: Now how did Cinderella finish her dress so fast? - Squirrels and birds. - That's not very helpful.
jokes.setup: Leslie desperately looking for dress solutions
jokes.punchline: Ann's literal fairy tale answer and Leslie's practical dismissal
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from Leslie's desperation to Ann's literal thinking

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Ann, I need a dress for the gala and I have nothing to wear.

dialogue.speaker: Ann
dialogue.line: A fairy godmother could appear and transform rags into a beautiful gown.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Ann, that's not helpful. I need an actual solution.

jokes.text: I forge government documents all the time. - That's true.
jokes.setup: Need to forge marriage license signature
jokes.punchline: April casually admitting to regular document forgery
jokes.explanation: Good April moment - casual admission of illegal activity

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: I can forge the signature. I do it all the time.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: What? Really?

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: Yeah, it's not that hard. I've forged my parents' signatures since I was twelve.

jokes.text: No time for verbs.
jokes.setup: Leslie frantically delegating tasks
jokes.punchline: Eliminating basic grammar to save time
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie efficiency taken to absurd extreme with great economy

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Ben, you take the permits. Ann, venue coordination. Tom, catering. Jerry, you're on... wait, what can Jerry do? Just stand there. Stand there, Jerry. Jerry stand.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: And stop talking in complete sentences everyone. No time. We abbreviate. 'The thing' instead of 'the thing that we discussed.' 'Donna did the stuff' instead of 'Donna completed the tasks assigned to her.' Pronouns optional. Articles eliminated. Verbs becoming suggestions.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: I'm not even sure words need to be real anymore. 'Ron spreadsheet the budget-ify.' Everyone understand? No? Good. Less time explaining, more time doing.

jokes.text: I should point out that you said 'duty' twice.
jokes.setup: Leslie frantically assigning duties
jokes.punchline: Ron's calm observation of her word choice
jokes.explanation: Mild Ron humor - practical observation during chaos

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: You just said 'touch base' and 'boil the ocean' in the same sentence. I'm not sure what you're trying to do, but I want no part of it.

jokes.text: There's a Li'l Sebastian look-alike on the way-- what? What? Oh, my G-- Oh! Oh.
jokes.setup: Donna excitedly announcing Li'l Sebastian surprise
jokes.punchline: Jerry's physical reaction (implied urination) from excitement
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Li'l Sebastian with Jerry's typical embarrassing reaction

dialogue.speaker: Donna
dialogue.line: We got Li'l Sebastian!

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: *looks shocked, then visibly pees himself*

jokes.text: Did you just pee your pants? Just a dab. You nasty, Jerry. Third time this week.
jokes.setup: Jerry's physical reaction to Li'l Sebastian news
jokes.punchline: Confirmation of urination and frequency revelation
jokes.explanation: Classic Jerry humiliation but relies on bodily function humor

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Did you just pee yourself?

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: A little bit.

dialogue.speaker: Donna
dialogue.line: Jerry, you pee yourself like once a week.

jokes.text: Your Minister Certification will be emailed to you in 24 hours. But this is America. I want it now!
jokes.setup: Tom trying to get ordained quickly online
jokes.punchline: Frustrated American entitlement attitude
jokes.explanation: Good cultural observation delivered through Tom's impatience

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: I've been on this website for twenty minutes and I still don't have my ordination certificate. What is this, communist Russia?

jokes.text: I had all these great bits written for the wedding too. I was gonna beatbox. There's a dance breakdown. I'm talking, like, six guaranteed applause breaks.
jokes.setup: Tom disappointed about not being able to officiate
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top performance plans for wedding ceremony
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom - turning solemn ceremony into entertainment spectacle

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: I was really looking forward to officiating your wedding. I had this whole thing planned out. I was gonna do a bit where I come down the aisle on a zip line.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Then I'm gonna do a backflip off the altar. Then I'm gonna pull out a flaming saxophone and just shred for like twenty minutes.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: And then at the end, I'm gonna say 'By the power vested in me by the state of Indiana and also by Nicki Minaj, I now pronounce you husband and wife.'

jokes.text: We open with Will Smith's monologue from the modern cinematic classic... Hitch.
jokes.setup: Tom describing his wedding ceremony plans
jokes.punchline: Treating Hitch as high art for wedding ceremony
jokes.explanation: Good Tom moment - pop culture reverence mixed with formal ceremony

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: I'm going to have Hitch playing at my wedding ceremony. It's high art.

jokes.text: - Turtledove. - There's your butt.
jokes.setup: Secret code words being used for wedding planning
jokes.punchline: Andy completely misunderstanding the code system
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy confusion with good economy of delivery

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Okay, so we have code words for the wedding. If I say 'waffles,' that means we need to talk about the venue.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Waffles! I love waffles!

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: No, Andy, it's a code word.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Right, right. So if you say 'waffles,' you want waffles.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: No, waffles means venue.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: A waffle venue? That's actually genius!

jokes.text: No rings, just a tennis bracelet and a necklace that says 'Wine Chick.'
jokes.setup: Looking for rings in Ann's belongings
jokes.punchline: Specific absurd jewelry finds
jokes.explanation: Good specific absurd details that feel realistic for Ann

jokes.text: Li'l Sebastian? No, no, sorry. Actually, this is Bucky. He's a, uh, licensed Li'l Sebastian impersonator.
jokes.setup: Everyone thinking they're seeing Li'l Sebastian
jokes.punchline: Reveal it's just an impersonator with professional licensing
jokes.explanation: Great callback with absurd detail about licensed impersonation

dialogue.speaker: Leslie Knope
dialogue.line: Oh my God, is that...? Li'l Sebastian!

dialogue.speaker: Ben Wyatt
dialogue.line: Wait, that can't be. Li'l Sebastian passed away.

dialogue.speaker: Horse handler
dialogue.line: Actually, I'm a certified Li'l Sebastian impersonator. I have my professional licensing and everything.

dialogue.speaker: Ron Swanson
dialogue.line: Professional licensing? For impersonating a horse?

dialogue.speaker: Horse handler
dialogue.line: It's a very specific skill set.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie Knope
dialogue.line: I... I can't believe this.

jokes.text: This is Li'l Sebastian, and this is a sign. We're gonna do this.
jokes.setup: Various wedding obstacles mounting up
jokes.punchline: Leslie treating fake Li'l Sebastian as divine intervention
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie optimism and Li'l Sebastian reverence combination

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Oh my god, is that... is that Li'l Sebastian?

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: This is a sign. This is the universe telling me that everything is going to be okay.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Li'l Sebastian has spoken.

jokes.text: There's always time. Designers, make it work.
jokes.setup: Ann referencing Project Runway for dress solution
jokes.punchline: Doing Tim Gunn impression despite time pressure
jokes.explanation: Solid pop culture reference moment for Ann

dialogue.speaker: Ann
dialogue.line: I'm going to channel my inner Tim Gunn. Make it work!

jokes.text: It's after 10:00. I've been asleep for four hours.
jokes.setup: Andy and April waking up city clerk at night
jokes.punchline: Revealing she goes to bed extremely early
jokes.explanation: Good character detail that feels authentic for elderly clerk

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: We need to wake up the city clerk. It's an emergency.

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: We've been knocking for ten minutes.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: What? What time is it?

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: It's 9 PM.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: 9 PM?! That's the middle of the night!

jokes.text: I'm not married. I was on a date tonight. It went well, he's sleeping over.
jokes.setup: Assuming the voice is her husband
jokes.punchline: Elderly woman casually mentioning hookup success
jokes.explanation: Great subversion of expectations with elderly character's casual sexuality

jokes.text: You have got an amazing house and a really cool nightgown and seem to be super good at dating.
jokes.setup: April trying to butter up the clerk
jokes.punchline: Awkward specific compliments about dating prowess
jokes.explanation: Good April awkwardness in trying to be nice

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: You know what? You have really nice hands. Like, really nice hands for holding someone's face while you kiss them.

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: And I bet you're great at knowing exactly when to text someone back. Not too eager, not too aloof.

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: Your shoulders probably look really good in a t-shirt. The kind of t-shirt that says 'I'm casual but also I care about my appearance.'

jokes.text: It's a toe ring with a brown gemstone? Is this a ruby that's gone bad?
jokes.setup: Looking through Ann's jewelry collection
jokes.punchline: Ben's confusion about questionable jewelry quality
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about questionable jewelry

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Is this... is this cubic zirconia?

dialogue.speaker: Ann
dialogue.line: It's a family heirloom.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Right, but it's fake, though.

jokes.text: How much do you think Ann would miss this sconce?
jokes.setup: Need rings desperately
jokes.punchline: Ron suggesting melting down Ann's light fixture
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron moment - practical solution that's completely destructive

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: We could melt down Ann's light fixture.

jokes.text: You ever heard Leslie talk about Ben's butt? I'm all like, 'damn, girl, you mad sweet on that back meat.'
jokes.setup: Tom practicing wedding speech material
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate sexual content for wedding ceremony
jokes.explanation: Tom's inappropriate humor - fits character but relies on crude content

jokes.text: Parks are stu-pid.
jokes.setup: Jamm protesting with megaphone
jokes.punchline: Childish pronunciation and argument against parks
jokes.explanation: Basic antagonist humor - childish but fits Jamm's character

dialogue.speaker: Jeremy Jamm
dialogue.line: Listen up! These so-called 'parks' are just wastelands for the lazy!

dialogue.speaker: Jeremy Jamm
dialogue.line: And another thing - they call it a 'park' but it's really a 'paaark'!

dialogue.speaker: Jeremy Jamm
dialogue.line: The government wants you thinking 'paaark' so you don't realize it's just dirt!

jokes.text: Hey, butter-teeth, what's up? I can fix those chompers for you. Just come to Jamm Orthodontics.
jokes.setup: Jamm being antagonistic at the wedding
jokes.punchline: Mixing insults with business promotion
jokes.explanation: Good Jamm character moment - obnoxious but consistent

dialogue.speaker: Jeremy Jamm
dialogue.line: You know what? This wedding is a joke, just like your department. But speaking of jokes, have you considered switching to Jamm & Associates for your legal needs? We offer competitive rates and a proven track record of success.

jokes.text: Terrible. And perfect.
jokes.setup: Leslie asking how the improvised dress looks
jokes.punchline: Ann's honest but supportive assessment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ann moment - brutally honest but ultimately supportive

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: How does it look?

dialogue.speaker: Ann
dialogue.line: It's... it's very you. I mean, it's not very flattering, but it's very you.

jokes.text: It is the letter from the statehouse... We were supposed to be here eight weeks. I'm so happy that those eight weeks turned into three years, and that you met Leslie, and that we both found a home.
jokes.setup: Chris giving Ben a meaningful wedding gift
jokes.punchline: Sweet sentiment about their unexpected Pawnee journey
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment with good callback to series history

dialogue.speaker: Chris
dialogue.line: Ben, when I first came to Pawnee, I thought it was the worst place on Earth. But then I met you, and Leslie, and everyone in this department. And I realized that Pawnee isn't a place—it's the people. You're my best friend, and I'm so grateful for this unexpected journey we've been on together.

jokes.text: It is like the Ann Perkins of dresses.
jokes.setup: Leslie seeing the finished dress
jokes.punchline: Using Ann as the ultimate compliment descriptor
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to Leslie's over-the-top Ann compliments as ultimate praise

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Oh my God, this dress is absolutely Ann.

jokes.text: I'm not supposed to see you before the wedding. - No, that's the groom - I ruined it!
jokes.setup: Leslie worried about bad luck traditions
jokes.punchline: Escalating panic about seeing the wrong person
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie panic escalation with wedding superstition confusion

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Wait, I can't see Ann before the meeting. That's bad luck!

dialogue.speaker: Ann
dialogue.line: Leslie, that's not a real thing.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Oh, you don't know that. What if I see Tom? That's double bad luck. What if I see Jerry? That's triple bad luck. What if I see all three of you at once? I think the building explodes.

dialogue.speaker: Ann
dialogue.line: The building doesn't explode.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: You don't know that!

jokes.text: ♪ Here comes the boo ♪ ♪ All dressed in boo ♪ ♪ Boo, Leslie Knope ♪
jokes.setup: Jamm disrupting the wedding ceremony
jokes.punchline: Childish parody of wedding march with 'boo'
jokes.explanation: Basic antagonist disruption - fits character but low craft

dialogue.speaker: Jeremy Jamm
dialogue.line: Boo! Boo! Boooo!

jokes.text: Amazon, baby, that's how. You are looking at a prime customer, everybody. Two days free shipping for this guy.
jokes.setup: Jamm throwing stink bombs at wedding
jokes.punchline: Bragging about Amazon Prime membership during disruption
jokes.explanation: Good absurd Jamm moment - bragging about mundane things during chaos

dialogue.speaker: Jeremy Jamm
dialogue.line: You know what? I have Amazon Prime. Two-day shipping, baby!

jokes.text: Well, good thing you're an orthodontist.
jokes.setup: Jamm complaining about his broken tooth
jokes.punchline: April's practical observation about his profession
jokes.explanation: Perfect April deadpan response to Jamm's whining

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: Well, you're a dentist. Isn't that a little ironic?

jokes.text: I lost my father when I was ten, I don't have any brothers, and Ken Burns never wrote me back. So I am not getting married without you there to walk me down the aisle.
jokes.setup: Leslie explaining why Ron must be at her wedding
jokes.punchline: Ken Burns documentary filmmaker rejection as third reason
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from tragic to absurd - perfectly Leslie to include Ken Burns in emotional moment

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Ron, you have to be at my wedding. First, you're my best friend. Second, I need someone to give me away who won't cry, and third... Ken Burns rejected you as a documentary filmmaker.

jokes.text: He chopped off both of his arms, so he wouldn't leave any fingerprints. Oh, he did? Well, how did he murder the person?
jokes.setup: Andy describing Jamm as psychopath mastermind
jokes.punchline: Chief pointing out logical flaw in armless murderer theory
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist logic failure - Andy's elaborate theory falls apart immediately

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: He's a psychopath mastermind. He's like an armless murderer.

dialogue.speaker: Chief Jim Dunn
dialogue.line: How would he murder anyone if he has no arms?

jokes.text: I got five bathrooms.
jokes.setup: Jamm trying to assert dominance while in jail
jokes.punchline: Bragging about bathroom quantity as measure of success
jokes.explanation: Running gag continuation - Jamm's odd wealth flexing

dialogue.speaker: Jeremy Jamm
dialogue.line: You know what? I've been in this cell for three hours, and I've already figured out the bathroom situation. There are TWO toilets on this block. TWO. Do you know how many bathrooms I have at home? Seventeen. SEVENTEEN. That's how you measure success in this world.

jokes.text: I love the first floor of City Hall at night. When all the fluorescents are half-off. Just makes the informational fliers look so beautiful.
jokes.setup: Leslie finding romance in government building
jokes.punchline: Finding beauty in mundane bureaucratic elements
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie moment - finding genuine beauty in bureaucratic settings

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: You know what I love about government buildings? The fluorescent lighting. It's so romantic.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Leslie, that's not romantic at all.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: And the beige walls? The filing cabinets? The faint smell of old paper and broken dreams? It's beautiful.

jokes.text: Jerry, everything I wrote is wrong. There's, like, 20 minutes of Hitch quotes before I even say Leslie's name. What do I do? You know what, Tom? Just speak from the heart, okay?
jokes.setup: Tom panicking about his over-elaborate wedding ceremony
jokes.punchline: Jerry giving unexpectedly good advice to speak from heart
jokes.explanation: Good character reversal - Jerry being wise while Tom overthinks

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: The ceremony is a disaster! I've got seventeen groomsmen, a string quartet playing remixes, and the aisle is lined with rose petals I had specially imported from—

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: Tom, just speak from the heart. That's all that matters.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Wait, that's... actually good advice.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: I know. Surprising, right?

jokes.text: I lived in villages with eight people
jokes.setup: Ben describing his work travel experience
jokes.punchline: Absurdly small community size
jokes.explanation: Solid detail about Ben's background with good specificity

jokes.text: this whole time, I was just wandering around, everywhere, just looking for you.
jokes.setup: Ben's professional travel experience
jokes.punchline: Romantic reframe as unconscious search for Leslie
jokes.explanation: Beautiful romantic payoff to setup - transforms practical into poetic

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: I've traveled to 47 different countries for work. I thought I was just doing my job, being a public servant, going where I was needed. But now I realize... I was unconsciously searching for Leslie the whole time.

jokes.text: the first draft of my vows, which I wrote the day after we got engaged, clocked in at around 70 pages.
jokes.setup: Leslie explaining her wedding vows situation
jokes.punchline: Comically excessive length for wedding vows
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie excess - turning simple vows into novel-length document

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: I've written my vows. They're about 247 pages long.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: 247 pages?

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: I have a lot of feelings about you, Ben. And I needed to express all of them. Alphabetically, chronologically, and by theme.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Leslie, the ceremony is only three hours long.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: I know. I've also written a 92-page appendix in case I miss anything.

jokes.text: You're all I need. I love you and I like you.
jokes.setup: Leslie giving heartfelt wedding vows
jokes.punchline: Including her signature 'and I like you' addition
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to Leslie's signature phrase in most meaningful context possible - economical and deeply character-driven

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Ben, you are my soulmate, my best friend, and the love of my life. And I like you.

jokes.text: Close only counts in horse grenades. It's a saying. Because if you're playing horseshoes, and then you throw a grenade at a horse, it doesn't have to be that close, and you can still blow the horse's legs off. It's from the movie Seabiscuit.
jokes.setup: Andy trying to comfort himself about failure
jokes.punchline: Completely mangled idiom with disturbing violent explanation
jokes.explanation: Excellent Andy absurdism - completely wrong idiom with disturbing logic and false movie reference

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: You know what they say... when life gives you lemons, you make lemonade. When life gives you melons, you make... melonade? Is that a thing?

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: No, wait, I got it. When one door closes, another door opens. Unless you're trapped in a room with no doors, then you have to gnaw through the drywall with your teeth until your gums bleed and you pass out from blood loss.

jokes.text: Chris can be very... Paternal.
jokes.setup: Chris giving intense life advice to Andy
jokes.punchline: Ben's understated observation about Chris's behavior
jokes.explanation: Good Ben understatement about Chris's intensity

dialogue.speaker: Chris
dialogue.line: Andy, you need to understand something. Life is about making choices. Real choices. Every single day you wake up, you're deciding who you want to be.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: He's been doing this for twenty minutes.

jokes.text: ♪ You trade your legs for angel's wings ♪
jokes.setup: Andy singing memorial song for Li'l Sebastian
jokes.punchline: Disturbing imagery of trading horse legs for wings
jokes.explanation: Good callback song with characteristically odd Andy lyrics

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: We'll remember Li'l Sebastian, always in our hearts. You brought us joy and laughter. And now you're gone... you're gone forever. We'll miss you, little buddy. Rest in peace.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I see you in a meadow, running free. But then... then your legs fall off and turn into wings. Beautiful, majestic wings. And you fly away, but something's not right. Something's deeply, deeply wrong.

jokes.text: Any moron with a crucible, an acetylene torch, and a cast-iron waffle maker could have done the same. Whole thing only took me about 20 minutes. People who buy things are suckers.
jokes.setup: Ron explaining how he made wedding rings from a light fixture
jokes.punchline: Dismissing incredibly complex metalworking as simple and universal
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron moment - dismissing master-level craftsmanship as basic skill everyone should have. Peak character comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: I just took apart the light fixture in my office, melted down the brass, and cast it into rings. It's not that hard. Any idiot can do it.

tmdb_overview: Leslie and Ben are getting married, and have to cram months of planning into two hours. Meanwhile, Ron's temper gets him in trouble.
insight: Romantic milestone trades laughs for warmth—62 jokes land softer than usual Parks fare.
